title: facility — declared entitlements
description: Five entitlement keys. `facilities` is the boolean feature gate for the whole module (off on Small tier per hierarchy.md §7). `facilities_advanced` gates the Enterprise-only surfaces (day_passes, passes, recurring bookings, blackouts, package integration). `facility_slot` caps the number of facilities per tenant. `booking_recurring` gates recurring-booking rule creation (Enterprise). `booking_capacity_gating` toggles per-tenant capacity enforcement (default off — gate exists so ops can flip it per-tenant without a code change).

entitlements.description: Master feature gate. Off = the whole module is invisible: no facility CRUD, no booking CRUD, no routes reachable. Small tier is locked out entirely per hierarchy.md §7.
enforcedBy: FacilityObserver refuses facility create when the gate is closed
SDUI resource surfaces hidden when features array does not include 'facilities.core'

entitlements.description: Enterprise-only advanced features: day_passes, passes, recurring bookings, blackouts, package integration. Medium tenants get basic facility + booking; Enterprise unlocks the rest.
enforcedBy: DayPassObserver refuses create when the gate is closed
PassObserver refuses create when the gate is closed
SDUI day-pass + pass resource surfaces hidden when features array does not include 'facilities.advanced'

entitlements.description: How many facilities a tenant can have. Small: 0 (feature off; slot is 0 for compliance with the gate). Medium: 20. Enterprise: unlimited (null cap). Consumed on facility create; released on soft-delete.

Enterprise-only. Recurring booking rules that auto-create N bookings from a template (e.g. 'every Tuesday 18:00–19:00 for 12 weeks'). Wave 2 ships the entitlement key + the SDUI toggle; the underlying `recurring_booking_rules` table lands in Wave 2b.

Enforces facility.capacity_persons when set. Default off in tests + ops-flip-off for tenants that want overbooking (rare — mostly gyms during peak with a waiver flow). When off, capacity is a UI hint only, not a hard block. Medium + Enterprise default on.
enforcedBy: ResourceBookingObserver.creating skips the capacity check when this entitlement is off
PricingCalculator still uses capacity_persons in its computations regardless
